summary: We are in full execution mode for the China Going-Global report and Intelligent China launch, with website redesign and SEO prioritization demanding immediate attention this week.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint report with IE Business School on Chinese companies going global, targeting a mid-September launch in Singapore; Felix is preparing a one-pager proposal and confirming event partnerships with Tech in Asia and Tyson. The Intelligent China program website must go live this week, with content optimization starting immediately, and the Asia Start campaign is running two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month. The knowledge website redesign is in review, with feedback due from Joseph and Julie on Joker's design, and a decision to combine elements from Joseph's editorial-style design. SEO focus is firmly on Asia Star and Intelligent China, deprioritizing US partnerships, with Joseph drafting initial search areas and conversion tracking setup. Urgent this week: finalize social media agency selection (Media Minds is favored), process UNICON membership invoice, and attend ad agency bidding presentations on July 15. Coming up: launch of the China Going-Global report, Intelligent China program rollout, and the CKGSB-EuroCham event on EV with Prof Li Wei.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: The team discussed worship of three three-story halls and later moved to their own location.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting it's similar to current site with improved menu categorization. They discussed combining elements from Joseph's more editorial-style design to show more articles and bring indices to the forefront.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on website menu, school brochure updates, CES quotes for social media, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency search. Concerns raised about Zhu Yang's ChatGPT-generated feedback and timeline for agency selection.

meetings.title: Joint Report on Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Team discusses co-publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a main research piece from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools. They plan to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, and Shenzhen.

decisions.text: Remove dedicated video section; videos will be dispersed by topic.
Keep knowledge menu centered rather than left-aligned.
Include indices (BCI, BSI) on the knowledge homepage.
Show more articles on homepage, with pinned article at top left and latest articles displayed.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; content optimization can begin immediately.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Zhu Yang to be blacklisted due to unproductive ChatGPT-generated feedback.
Media Minds likely best agency given small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
